stem: A media company needs to store video transcoding jobs in a queue and process them one at a time, in strict first-in-first-out order. Which SQS queue type is required?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon SNS topic — delivers in order to all subscribers

options.label: B
options.text: SQS Standard queue — highest throughput, best-effort ordering

options.label: C
options.text: SQS FIFO queue — preserves message order and ensures exactly-once processing

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon EventBridge — routes events in order to targets

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Kinesis — ensures ordered stream processing

explanation: SQS FIFO queues guarantee that messages are processed in the exact order they are sent and each message is delivered exactly once. Required for use cases where job processing order matters (transcoding queue, financial transactions, etc.).

stem: An application tier consists of 6 EC2 instances behind an ALB. Two instances are in us-east-1a and four are in us-east-1b. If us-east-1a loses all connectivity, what happens to application availability?

options.label: A
options.text: The application goes down because 2 of 6 instances are unreachable

options.label: B
options.text: The application fails over to a standby Region automatically

options.label: C
options.text: Auto Scaling immediately launches 2 replacement instances in us-east-1a

options.label: D
options.text: The ALB continues routing all traffic to the 4 healthy instances in us-east-1b

options.label: E
options.text: The ALB splits traffic 50/50 regardless of AZ health

explanation: The ALB continuously health-checks all registered targets. When us-east-1a instances fail their checks, the ALB removes them from rotation and routes all traffic to the 4 healthy us-east-1b instances — the application remains available.

stem: A startup wants to build a mobile app backend that handles authentication, data storage, and real-time updates without managing servers. Which AWS service is purpose-built for mobile and web backends?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon S3 static website with Lambda

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Elastic Beanstalk with a Java backend

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon EC2 with a Node.js server

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon RDS with a REST API on EC2

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Amplify / AWS AppSync with DynamoDB

explanation: AWS Amplify provides frontend hosting and CI/CD; AppSync provides a managed GraphQL API with real-time subscriptions; Cognito handles authentication; DynamoDB handles data — together these form a fully managed, serverless mobile/web backend.

stem: A company runs a large-scale Hadoop data processing cluster on-premises. They want to migrate to AWS and still use Hadoop-compatible processing frameworks without managing the cluster nodes themselves. Which service should they use?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Glue is a fully managed extract, transform, and load service that makes it easy to prepare and load data for analytics.

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce)

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Redshift is a fast, fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service that makes it simple and cost-effective to analyze your data.

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that provides fast and predictable performance with seamless scalability.

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics

distractors.A: AWS Glue is a fully managed extract, transform, and load service that makes it easy to prepare and load data for analytics.
distractors.C: Amazon Redshift is a fast, fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service that makes it simple and cost-effective to analyze your data.
distractors.D: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that provides fast and predictable performance with seamless scalability.

explanation: Amazon EMR is a managed service for running big data frameworks including Apache Hadoop, Spark, Hive, and HBase. AWS manages the cluster provisioning and configuration — customers just run their jobs.

stem: An EC2 instance running a web server needs to access an S3 bucket in the same Region. Which option provides the most secure, low-cost path without routing traffic over the internet?

options.label: A
options.text: Use S3 Transfer Acceleration for low-latency access

options.label: B
options.text: Configure a NAT Gateway for the private subnet to reach S3

options.label: C
options.text: Assign a public IP to the EC2 instance and use S3 over HTTPS

options.label: D
options.text: Use a VPC Gateway Endpoint for S3 — traffic stays within AWS network

options.label: E
options.text: Use Direct Connect to route all S3 traffic through your data center

explanation: A VPC Gateway Endpoint for S3 routes traffic from EC2 to S3 entirely within the AWS private network — no internet gateway, NAT gateway, or public IP required. There is no data transfer charge for traffic through the endpoint.

stem: A development team wants to build a chatbot that understands natural language questions about their product catalog. Which AWS service provides a managed natural language understanding framework for building conversational interfaces?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Comprehend — extracts entities and sentiment from text

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Rekognition — analyzes images for object detection

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Lex — builds conversational interfaces with NLU and ASR

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Transcribe — converts speech to text

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Polly — converts text to speech

explanation: Amazon Lex provides the same deep learning technologies that power Alexa — automatic speech recognition (ASR) and natural language understanding (NLU). It manages intent recognition, dialog flows, and session state for building chatbots.

stem: A company's application writes data to Amazon S3 and uses Amazon Athena to query it with SQL. Their S3 data is stored as JSON but queries are slow. Which data format change would most improve Athena query performance and reduce cost?

options.label: A
options.text: Convert data to Parquet or ORC — columnar formats optimized for analytical queries

options.label: B
options.text: Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering — queries run faster on frequently accessed objects

options.label: C
options.text: Use S3 Replication to create a read replica of the data

options.label: D
options.text: Move data to RDS — relational databases are faster than S3 for SQL

options.label: E
options.text: Convert data to CSV — simpler format for SQL engines

explanation: Athena charges per TB of data scanned. Columnar formats (Parquet, ORC) store data column-by-column — Athena reads only the columns needed for each query, dramatically reducing scan volume, improving speed, and cutting cost.

stem: A company wants to create a serverless data pipeline that automatically extracts data from S3, transforms it, and loads it into Redshift — all without managing ETL servers. Which service is purpose-built for this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Lambda — custom ETL code triggered by S3 events

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose — streams data in real time

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon EMR — managed Hadoop cluster for ETL

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Glue — serverless ETL with automatic schema discovery

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Redshift Spectrum — queries data in S3 directly

explanation: AWS Glue is a fully managed serverless ETL service. It crawls data sources, discovers schemas, and generates ETL code that can transform and load data between S3, Redshift, RDS, and other targets — no server provisioning needed.

stem: A company hosts an e-commerce website. During checkout, customers select shipping options that require calling a third-party shipping API. If the shipping API is slow, the checkout page hangs. Which architectural change would prevent the shipping API's slowness from affecting the checkout experience?

options.label: A
options.text: Place an SQS queue between checkout and the shipping API call

options.label: B
options.text: Move the shipping API call to a Lambda function

options.label: C
options.text: Increase the timeout on the checkout page to 60 seconds

options.label: D
options.text: Add more EC2 instances to the checkout service

options.label: E
options.text: Cache the shipping API response in ElastiCache

explanation: SQS decouples the checkout (writes order to queue, completes immediately) from the shipping API call (processed asynchronously). The customer receives a confirmation instantly — shipping details are updated moments later without blocking the checkout flow.

stem: A software team uses multiple AWS accounts and wants a single place to view all security findings, compliance scores, and threat detections across every account. Which service provides this unified security view?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Security Hub — aggregates security findings from multiple services.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Config — aggregates configuration compliance across accounts

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon GuardDuty — provides a multi-account threat dashboard

options.label: D
options.text: AWS CloudTrail — aggregates API logs across accounts

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon CloudWatch — aggregates metrics across accounts

explanation: AWS Security Hub acts as the central aggregation point for security findings across accounts and services. It ingests from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, Config, IAM Access Analyzer, and integrated third-party tools into a single prioritized dashboard.

stem: A company wants to send transactional emails (order confirmations, password resets) to millions of customers from their AWS-hosted application. Which AWS service is designed for bulk and transactional email delivery?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Pinpoint — sends push notifications to mobile apps

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Lambda — sends emails using the SMTP protocol

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon SNS — sends SMS and email notifications

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon SQS — queues email messages for processing

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon SES (Simple Email Service) — managed email sending for high-volume delivery

explanation: Amazon SES is a cloud-based email sending service designed for transactional and marketing emails at scale. It provides high deliverability, bounce/complaint handling, DKIM/SPF support, and dedicated IP options.

stem: A company uses AWS CloudFormation to manage infrastructure. A new developer accidentally deletes a production CloudFormation stack, which destroys the database. Which CloudFormation feature should have been enabled to prevent this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Config — records stack deletion events

options.label: B
options.text: CloudFormation Change Sets — requires review before applying changes

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Backup — backs up CloudFormation templates

options.label: D
options.text: CloudFormation Termination Protection — prevents a stack from being deleted

options.label: E
options.text: CloudFormation Rollback — reverts failed deployments

explanation: CloudFormation Termination Protection, when enabled on a stack, prevents the stack from being accidentally deleted. Any delete attempt returns an error — the protection must be explicitly disabled before deletion can proceed.

stem: A company needs to extract text and data from millions of scanned PDF forms to populate a database. Which AWS service automates this without requiring custom ML code?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Glue — crawls PDFs and extracts structured data

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Transcribe — converts speech in PDFs to text

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Rekognition — detects text in images

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Textract — extracts text, tables, and form data from documents

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Comprehend — extracts entities from text

explanation: Amazon Textract uses ML to extract printed text, handwriting, tables, and form fields from scanned documents and PDFs. It returns structured output including key-value pairs from forms — ideal for document processing pipelines.

stem: A company runs EC2 instances that must comply with a licensing requirement allowing only a specific number of sockets. They need to track which physical servers their instances run on. Which EC2 option provides this visibility and control?

options.label: A
options.text: EC2 Auto Scaling groups — track instance distribution

options.label: B
options.text: EC2 Dedicated Hosts — provides visibility into the specific physical

options.label: C
options.text: Placement Groups — control instance placement in a cluster

options.label: D
options.text: Reserved Instances — specify instance type

options.label: E
options.text: EC2 Spot Instances — run on unused capacity of any host

explanation: EC2 Dedicated Hosts give you a dedicated physical server with full visibility into the number of sockets, cores, and host IDs. This is essential for BYOL (Bring Your Own License) software with per-socket or per-core licensing.

stem: A company uses multiple microservices, each with complex dependencies and retry logic. They want to orchestrate these services into a workflow with error handling, retries, and branching — without building custom orchestration code. Which service handles this?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon EventBridge — routes events between services

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon SNS — fans out messages to all services

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Step Functions — visual serverless workflow orchestration

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon SQS — chains queues between services

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon MQ — broker for messaging between services

explanation: AWS Step Functions orchestrates Lambda functions and other AWS services into visual workflows with built-in state management, branching, parallel execution, retry logic, and error handling — removing the need for custom orchestration code.

stem: A company stores 500 TB of data on-premises and wants to extend this to AWS, using AWS for overflow capacity while keeping frequently accessed data on-premises. Which service provides seamless on-premises to cloud storage extension?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Snowball — ships data to AWS

options.label: B
options.text: AWS DataSync — migrates data to S3

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Storage Gateway — presents AWS storage as local

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Direct Connect — provides a private network to AWS

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon EFS — provides a network file system

explanation: AWS Storage Gateway presents AWS storage (S3, S3 Glacier, EBS) as standard local storage protocols (NFS, SMB, iSCSI). Applications continue using local protocols — Gateway caches hot data on-premises and stores cold data in AWS automatically.

stem: A company wants to run their application containers in AWS and requires Kubernetes. They want the underlying nodes to be managed by AWS so they don't have to patch worker nodes. Which approach achieves this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Batch — managed container job scheduling

options.label: B
options.text: EKS with self-managed EC2 node groups — customer manages nodes

options.label: C
options.text: ECS with EC2 launch type — customer manages cluster nodes

options.label: D
options.text: EKS with EKS Managed Node Groups — AWS handles node provisioning.

options.label: E
options.text: EC2 instances with kubeadm installation — full self-managed Kubernetes

explanation: EKS Managed Node Groups let AWS handle node provisioning, AMI updates, and draining/terminating nodes for updates — the customer specifies desired configuration and AWS manages the worker node lifecycle.

stem: An application must translate user-submitted text between 75 languages in real time. The team has no ML expertise. Which fully managed AWS service handles this?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Lex — understands multiple languages in conversation

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Comprehend — detects language but doesn't translate

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Translate — neural machine translation between languages

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Polly — converts text to speech in multiple languages

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon SageMaker — train a custom translation model

explanation: Amazon Translate is a neural machine translation service supporting 75+ languages. It integrates with a simple API call — no ML expertise or model training required.

stem: A company needs to run a database that handles complex graph relationships (social connections, fraud detection networks). Which AWS database service is designed for graph data?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon ElastiCache — in-memory caching for relationships

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Neptune — managed graph database

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon DynamoDB — NoSQL key-value and document store

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Redshift — data warehouse for analytics

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon RDS — relational database for structured data

explanation: Amazon Neptune is a fully managed graph database supporting Gremlin and SPARQL query languages. It is optimized for querying highly connected datasets like social networks, recommendation engines, and fraud detection graphs.

stem: A company's application logs to Amazon CloudWatch Logs and they want to search for error patterns, create metrics from log data, and set alarms. Which CloudWatch feature enables this?

options.label: A
options.text: CloudWatch Synthetics — synthetic monitoring canaries

options.label: B
options.text: CloudWatch Metric Filters — extract metric data from log events

options.label: C
options.text: CloudWatch Contributor Insights — identify top contributors to traffic

options.label: D
options.text: CloudWatch Container Insights — monitor container performance

options.label: E
options.text: CloudWatch Dashboards — visualize metrics in graphs

explanation: CloudWatch Metric Filters parse log events in CloudWatch Logs, extract numeric values matching a pattern (e.g., count ERROR occurrences), and publish them as custom CloudWatch metrics that can then be used in alarms and dashboards.

stem: A company runs a three-tier application. The web tier EC2 instances in a public subnet need to communicate with the app tier in a private subnet. What controls the traffic flow between these two subnets?

options.label: A
options.text: Elastic IPs — assign public IPs to private subnet instances

options.label: B
options.text: VPC Peering — connect public and private subnets

options.label: C
options.text: Route tables — determine where subnet traffic is directed

options.label: D
options.text: Direct Connect — private connectivity between subnets

options.label: E
options.text: Internet Gateway — routes traffic between subnets

explanation: Within a VPC, traffic between subnets is routed by the VPC router using route tables (subnets in the same VPC can communicate by default). Security groups on the app tier instances control which source IPs and ports are allowed — typically only from the web tier's security group.

stem: A company wants to provide their customers with an interactive voice response (IVR) system using AWS. Customers call a phone number, speak their request, and the system responds. Which two services are core to this solution?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Lambda (custom voice processing) and Amazon SQS (queue calls)

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Connect (cloud contact center) with Amazon Lex — managed telephony with NLU

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon SES (email) and Amazon SNS (SMS notifications)

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Polly (text to speech) and Amazon Rekognition (voice analysis)

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Transcribe (speech to text) and Amazon Lex (NLU and dialog) — voice input to intelligent IVR

explanation: Amazon Connect is a managed cloud contact center that handles telephony (phone numbers, call routing). It integrates natively with Amazon Lex for NLU and intent-based IVR — together providing a fully managed intelligent phone system.

stem: A company's Amazon RDS database storage is 80% full and growing. Which RDS feature ensures storage grows automatically without downtime or manual intervention?

options.label: A
options.text: RDS Multi-AZ — standby replica takes over when primary is full

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Aurora Serverless — auto-scales compute and storage simultaneously

options.label: C
options.text: RDS Read Replicas — additional instances absorb write overflow

options.label: D
options.text: RDS Storage Auto Scaling — automatically increases storage when utilization is high

options.label: E
options.text: RDS Snapshot Restore — restore to a larger instance when full

explanation: RDS Storage Auto Scaling monitors free storage and automatically scales up storage capacity when it reaches a configured threshold — without any downtime or manual intervention from the customer.

stem: A company's disaster recovery plan requires their application to be restored in a different AWS Region within 4 hours if the primary Region becomes unavailable. Which approach achieves this?

options.label: A
options.text: Pilot Light — keep minimal infrastructure running in the DR Region.

options.label: B
options.text: Store backups in S3 Standard in the primary Region

options.label: C
options.text: Enable CloudTrail in the DR Region — logs provide recovery information

options.label: D
options.text: Deploy identical infrastructure in both Regions and route traffic equally

options.label: E
options.text: Multi-AZ deployment — deploys across AZs in the same Region

explanation: Pilot Light keeps core components (database replication, pre-built AMIs) running in the DR Region. During a disaster, you scale up the pre-built components to full capacity — achievable in under 4 hours at lower ongoing cost than full active-active.

stem: A company wants to build a data lake on AWS to store raw data from multiple sources including S3, RDS, and DynamoDB, and make it queryable with SQL. Which combination of services forms this architecture?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon EC2 with Hadoop + Amazon S3 + Amazon EMR

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon RDS + Amazon Redshift + Amazon DynamoDB

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon S3 (storage) + AWS Glue (catalog + ETL) +

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon S3 + Amazon CloudFront + Amazon Route 53

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon DynamoDB + Amazon ElastiCache + Amazon Kinesis

explanation: The serverless AWS data lake architecture: S3 stores raw and curated data, Glue crawls sources and maintains the schema catalog, and Athena queries S3 data directly with SQL using the Glue catalog — all serverless, no infrastructure to manage.

stem: A company needs to monitor their entire AWS infrastructure health, including EC2 instance status, ELB request counts, and custom application metrics — all in a single customizable dashboard. Which service provides this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — monitors infrastructure best practices

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Health Dashboard — shows service status for all Regions

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Systems Manager — manages infrastructure operations

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Config — records resource configuration state

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon CloudWatch — collects metrics, logs, and events.

explanation: Amazon CloudWatch collects metrics from AWS services (EC2, ELB, RDS) and custom application sources. CloudWatch Dashboards provide customizable, shareable views of these metrics in real-time graphs.

stem: A global e-commerce company needs their checkout flow to have sub-50ms database response times for customers worldwide. Which database architecture should they consider?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon RDS Read Replicas in each Region with manual failover

options.label: B
options.text: DynamoDB in one Region with ElastiCache

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Aurora Global Database — replicates to multiple Regions.

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Redshift in one Region with CloudFront in front

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon RDS with Multi-AZ in one Region

explanation: Aurora Global Database spans multiple Regions with under 1-second replication lag. Secondary Regions serve reads with local latency. If the primary Region fails, a secondary can be promoted with minimal downtime — enabling truly global database access.

stem: A company wants to automatically apply OS patches to hundreds of EC2 instances on a defined schedule without logging into each instance individually. Which AWS service provides centralized patch management?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Config — remediates non-compliant patch levels automatically

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager — automates OS patching across instances

options.label: C
options.text: AWS CloudFormation — redeploy instances with new AMIs

options.label: D
options.text: EC2 Image Builder — creates new AMIs with patches applied

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Inspector — scans for missing patches and applies them

explanation: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager automates the patching of EC2 instances (and on-premises servers) at scale. You define patch baselines and maintenance windows — SSM applies approved patches on schedule without requiring SSH access.

stem: A solutions architect is designing a cost-effective architecture for a startup. Which TWO AWS services allow them to run code or containers without paying for idle server time? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon EC2 On-Demand — pay per hour while running

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Fargate — runs containers serverlessly; billed per vCPU and memory consumed

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon RDS — managed relational database

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon EBS — block storage billed per GB provisioned

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Lambda — runs functions in response to events; billed per invocation and duration

explanation: Lambda charges only when code is executing (per-millisecond billing). Fargate charges per vCPU/memory second consumed during container execution. Neither charges for idle time — they are true pay-per-use compute services.

stem: A company is evaluating AWS database services for different use cases. Which TWO statements correctly match a service to its primary use case? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon RDS — best for storing images and video files

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Redshift — best for OLTP banking transactions requiring immediate consistency

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon ElastiCache — best for reducing repeated read queries from a relational database

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon DynamoDB — best for a product catalog requiring single-digit millisecond reads at any scale

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Neptune — best for storing large volumes of log files

explanation: DynamoDB's NoSQL architecture delivers consistent low-latency reads regardless of scale — ideal for catalog lookups. ElastiCache caches frequently accessed query results in memory, reducing database load and response time for repeated reads.
